name: node admin (fleet backend)
summary: The node-side management surface a urirun-fleet reconcile plan drives: rich runtime state, registry rebuild, restart (drop stale workers), atomic upgrade/rollback, and capability smoke — all as admin-gated node:// routes.
description: This connector is the backend for fleet reconciliation. A host computes drift and emits node:// remedy URIs; this connector executes them ON the node: runtime/query/state reports the ground-truth version/registry/lockfile a host diffs against desired state; registry/command/rebuild recompiles a registry built from stale bindings; runtime/command/restart (and worker/command/reload) restart the service to drop stale warm-worker imports; runtime/command/upgrade builds a fresh release OFF to the side (venv + deps + registry), smoke-tests it, and only then flips the current symlink and restarts (atomic — a failed build/smoke never touches the running node); runtime/command/rollback swaps back to the previous release; smoke/command/run checks required routes are present. Atomic releases reuse urirun-fleet's ReleaseManager. These are ADMIN operations — the node must gate node:// behind an enrolled key / token.
useCases: Let a host fix a stale node over the mesh: rebuild registry, restart, verify — no SSH.
Upgrade a node atomically (build+smoke off to the side, switch only on pass), roll back on failure.
Report lockfile-grade runtime state so the fleet can diff desired vs actual accurately.
